title: mountpoints left after running container with userns=auto
body:
### Issue Description

I scanned some existing reports but didn't see anything that looks like what I seem to see.

When I run containers with userns=auto in containers.conf then when the container run is over I see mountpoints 'left behind' on the system for an overlayfs - the container is gone, there is no data in the mount point.
The container has executed successfully.

```
# podman --version
podman version 5.7.1

# this is an example of what is left behind if I run an alpine container with --rm and command 'sleep 10'
overlay on /var/volatile/tmp/intermediate-mountpoint-0.0 type overlay (rw,nosuid,nodev,relatime,lowerdir=/containers/storage/overlay/97d538e6716f2cf1c5bc1e0f3157e52fcbaeb783a8d1ae41e0ea1e153096fad4/mapped/0/l/EKYIWOVL57PDRT7COM7UUQSSL3,upperdir=/containers/storage/overlay/97d538e6716f2cf1c5bc1e0f3157e52fcbaeb783a8d1ae41e0ea1e153096fad4/diff,workdir=/containers/storage/overlay/97d538e6716f2cf1c5bc1e0f3157e52fcbaeb783a8d1ae41e0ea1e153096fad4/work,uuid=on,metacopy=on)
```
Has anyone else seen this?
thanks,
Tom

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.run alpine container, allow it to complete
2.verify the container is gone
3.check the mount table


### Describe the results you received

A 'stray' mount remains

### Describe the results you expected

I expect the mount to be gone and the mountpoint to be deleted

### podman info output

```yaml
podman version 5.7.1
linux kernel 6.12
arm64
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Hello, 
1. could you please check this with the latest release `v5.8.0` ?
2. Please post full output of `podman info`

Thanks for checking.
I just experimented and the issue is reproducible with v5.8.0.
```
# podman ps --all
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
#
# mount | grep overlay
overlay on /var/volatile/tmp/intermediate-mountpoint-0.0 type overlay (rw,nosuid,nodev,relatime,lowerdir=/containers/storage/overlay/011143a6e6619b0e0b35438fae3de571f1034b662dbcd15e119bd38f30304998/mapped/0/l/HX4Q36OTFM5XJVRGTVUTJLUAPI:/containers/storage/overlay/011143a6e6619b0e0b35438fae3de571f1034b662dbcd15e119bd38f30304998/mapped/0/l/JLBY6ILUQXZINRKGZ2BMOEOFUP:/containers/storage/overlay/011143a6e6619b0e0b35438fae3de571f1034b662dbcd15e119bd38f30304998/mapped/0/l/BHVW3DE2DYU5XAEMKWZO2UV35X:/containers/storage/overlay/011143a6e6619b0e0b35438fae3de571f1034b662dbcd15e119bd38f30304998/mapped/0/l/226GUDS56ZVASZ443DQWK6MMMI:/containers/storage/overlay/011143a6e6619b0e0b35438fae3de571f1034b662dbcd15e119bd38f30304998/mapped/0/l/UMM5C4HYTPFFNXYY6XYBDGBO5T,upperdir=/containers/storage/overlay/011143a6e6619b0e0b35438fae3de571f1034b662dbcd15e119bd38f30304998/diff,workdir=/containers/storage/overlay/011143a6e6619b0e0b35438fae3de571f1034b662dbcd15e119bd38f30304998/work,uuid=on,metacopy=on)
# ls -al /var/volatile/tmp/intermediate-mountpoint-0.0/
total 0
# ls -al /var/volatile/tmp/
total 4
drwxrwxrwt    6 root     root           120 Mar 10 11:03 .
drwxrwxrwt    4 root     root            80 Mar 10 10:51 ..
dr-xr-xr-x    1 100000   100000           0 Mar 10 11:04 intermediate-mountpoint-0.0
```

Here's some version bits from podman info (will attach file) on my test setup
```

  arch: arm64
  buildahVersion: 1.43.0
  
  conmon:
    version: 'conmon version 2.1.13, commit: 42da7ac3fe667a3369d54413ef92664043105f19'
 
  kernel: 6.12.62-yocto-standard-gab3c2ff19f12
  
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: Unknown
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 1.24.0.0.0.19-6461-dirty
      commit: 64611d7ac938b8397e8a00a0e69987583fadec7d
 
  os: linux
  
store:
  configFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 0
    paused: 0
    running: 0
    stopped: 0
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions:
    overlay.mountopt: nodev,metacopy=on,nosuid
  graphRoot: /containers/storage
  graphRootAllocated: 510873600
  graphRootUsed: 3428352
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Supports shifting: "true"
    Supports volatile: "true"
    Using metacopy: "true"
  imageCopyTmpDir: /var/tmp
  imageStore:
    number: 0
  runRoot: /run/containers/storage
  transientStore: false
  volumePath: /containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 5.8.0
  Built: 1770910886
  BuiltTime: Thu Feb 12 15:41:26 2026
  GitCommit: 07efc23e05c3d9aa15a0f30d57194737bfc4b6b1
  GoVersion: go1.25.4
  Os: linux
  OsArch: linux/arm64
  Version: 5.8.0
```
and some config
```
# cat /etc/subuid
containers:100000:65536
# cat /etc/subgid
containers:100000:65536
# grep userns /etc/containers/containers.conf
userns = "auto"
```
everything works 'as expected' except the remaining mount.
I wonder if cleanupStorage() in libpod/container_internal.go might need an extra check for these intermediate mounts?

I'm unable to reproduce this on Fedora x86_64 and aarch64. I wonder if it's an issue with your Yocto setup.

Can you run: 
```
podman --log-level=debug run --rm --userns=auto alpine echo hello 2>&1 | grep -iE 'unmount|cleanup|intermediate|error'
```

Curious about a couple of other things:
1. Your crun seems to be about 4 months old, going by commit ID. Could you try using a newer crun?
2. Does your yocto setup use systemd or something else?
Thanks for the extra testing, and yes it could be the config on the system, but I can't see the immediate culprit....I will try that test.

I took the latest crun I could find, 'c07aadc344aacc867c1a334e5c0c1914da13365c', and I still see the vestige mount at the end.
Yes the yocto build uses systemd: podman is running as a systemd service and we're making api calls.

I ran the podman command you described above as well - it also shows a mountpoint left on the system
```
# podman --log-level=debug run --rm --userns=auto 1ab49c echo hello 2>&1 | grep -iE 'unmount|cleanup|intermediate|error'
time="2026-03-13T11:23:05Z" level=debug msg="running conmon: /usr/bin/conmon" args="[--api-version 1 -c 4ef09543161726fd00365b4a70b3ae5bc79947f896b632e1aa390ce5529fe2f8 -u 4ef09543161726fd00365b4a70b3ae5bc79947f896b632e1aa390ce5529fe2f8 -r /usr/bin/crun -b /containers/storage/overlay-containers/4ef09543161726fd00365b4a70b3ae5bc79947f896b632e1aa390ce5529fe2f8/userdata -p /run/containers/storage/overlay-containers/4ef09543161726fd00365b4a70b3ae5bc79947f896b632e1aa390ce5529fe2f8/userdata/pidfile -n musing_goldwasser --exit-dir /run/libpod/exits --persist-dir /run/libpod/persist/4ef09543161726fd00365b4a70b3ae5bc79947f896b632e1aa390ce5529fe2f8 --full-attach -s -l journald --log-level debug --syslog --conmon-pidfile /run/containers/storage/overlay-containers/4ef09543161726fd00365b4a70b3ae5bc79947f896b632e1aa390ce5529fe2f8/userdata/conmon.pid --no-pivot --exit-command /usr/bin/podman --exit-command-arg --root --exit-command-arg /containers/storage --exit-command-arg --runroot --exit-command-arg /run/containers/storage --exit-command-arg --log-level --exit-command-arg debug --exit-command-arg --exit-command-arg netavark --exit-command-arg --volumepath --exit-command-arg /containers/storage/volumes --exit-command-arg --db-backend --exit-command-arg sqlite --exit-command-arg --transient-store=false --exit-command-arg --hooks-dir --exit-command-arg /usr/share/containers/oci/hooks.d --exit-command-arg --runtime --exit-command-arg crun --exit-command-arg --storage-driver --exit-command-arg overlay --exit-command-arg --storage-opt --exit-command-arg overlay.mountopt=nodev,metacopy=on,nosuid --exit-command-arg --events-backend --exit-command-arg journald --exit-command-arg --syslog --exit-command-arg container --exit-command-arg cleanup --exit-command-arg --stopped-only --exit-command-arg --rm --exit-command-arg 4ef09543161726fd00365b4a70b3ae5bc79947f896b632e1aa390ce5529fe2f8]"
time="2026-03-13T11:23:05Z" level=debug msg="Unmounted container \"4ef09543161726fd00365b4a70b3ae5bc79947f896b632e1aa390ce5529fe2f8\""
time="2026-03-13T11:23:05Z" level=debug msg="Container 4ef09543161726fd00365b4a70b3ae5bc79947f896b632e1aa390ce5529fe2f8 storage is already unmounted, skipping..."
time="2026-03-13T11:23:05Z" level=debug msg="Container 4ef09543161726fd00365b4a70b3ae5bc79947f896b632e1aa390ce5529fe2f8 storage is already unmounted, skipping..."
Error: conmon failed: exit status 1
```
If you run a `podman` command with `--log-level=debug` and check Syslog, do you see any errors there? I suspect the Podman cleanup process is not running properly.

all logs go to systemd journal but nothing additional by way of errors to see
```
Mar 23 16:37:14 gsc podman[1496]: 2026-03-23 16:37:14.492769182 +0000 UTC m=+9.810857844 container start 3f34a0be60f6ddfddbfeb05badfc9fd9d48899615d064691d5b2cf886e26bb10 (image=docker.io/library/alpine:3.23.2, name=reverent_fermat)
Mar 23 16:37:45 gsc podman[1565]: 2026-03-23 16:37:45.848356773 +0000 UTC m=+0.880734133 container died 3f34a0be60f6ddfddbfeb05badfc9fd9d48899615d064691d5b2cf886e26bb10 (image=docker.io/library/alpine:3.23.2, name=reverent_fermat)

Mar 23 16:37:50 gsc systemd[1]: containers-storage-overlay\x2dcontainers-3f34a0be60f6ddfddbfeb05badfc9fd9d48899615d064691d5b2cf886e26bb10-userdata-shm.mount: Deactivated successfully.
Mar 23 16:37:50 gsc systemd[1]: containers-storage-overlay-3b1fbea53208c1051da81d562fd7015c9f6cdeb289945b6578c4f245a5c59b4c-merged.mount: Deactivated successfully.
Mar 23 16:37:51 gsc podman[1565]: 2026-03-23 16:37:51.564914702 +0000 UTC m=+6.597297202 container remove 3f34a0be60f6ddfddbfeb05badfc9fd9d48899615d064691d5b2cf886e26bb10 (image=docker.io/library/alpine:3.23.2, name=reverent_fermat)
Mar 23 16:37:51 gsc systemd[1]: containers-storage-overlay.mount: Deactivated successfully.
```
Yes I think the cleanup isnt happening and I cant see where it is triggered in the code
I could rebuild podman with extra debug if it helped, what would be useful?
Sorry, you need `--syslog` as well - so `--log-level=debug --syslog` - that should print full logs from the cleanup process, which runs in the background after container exit, to the journal.

I see this, but mount remains
```
$ podman run --detach --rm --syslog --log-level=debug e8f9ca9f1870 sleep 30

Mar 23 17:48:26 gsc /usr/bin/podman[2211]: time="2026-03-23T17:48:26Z" level=debug msg="Successfully cleaned up container 09b71f7e747b41d3f488f29f6fc18b078817b392a141a051a215e7379e274c02"
Mar 23 17:48:26 gsc systemd[1]: containers-storage-overlay\x2dcontainers-09b71f7e747b41d3f488f29f6fc18b078817b392a141a051a215e7379e274c02-userdata-shm.mount: Deactivated successfully.
Mar 23 17:48:26 gsc /usr/bin/podman[2211]: time="2026-03-23T17:48:26Z" level=debug msg="Unmounted container \"09b71f7e747b41d3f488f29f6fc18b078817b392a141a051a215e7379e274c02\""

Mar 23 17:48:27 gsc /usr/bin/podman[2211]: time="2026-03-23T17:48:27Z" level=debug msg="Called cleanup.PersistentPostRunE(/usr/bin/podman --root /containers/storage --runroot /run/containers/storage --log-level debug
--cgroup-manager systemd --tmpdir /run/libpod --network-config-dir  --network-backend netavark --volumepath /containers/storage/volumes --db-backend sqlite --transient-store=false --hooks-dir /usr/share/containers/oci/hooks.d
--runtime crun --storage-driver overlay --storage-opt overlay.mountopt=nodev,metacopy=on,nosuid --events-backend journald --syslog container cleanup --stopped-only --rm 09b71f7e747b41d3f488f29f6fc18b078817b392a141a051a215e7379e274c02)"
Mar 23 17:48:27 gsc /usr/bin/podman[2211]: time="2026-03-23T17:48:27Z" level=debug msg="Shutting down engines"
Mar 23 17:48:27 gsc /usr/bin/podman[2211]: time="2026-03-23T17:48:27Z" level=info msg="Received shutdown.Stop(), terminating!" PID=2211
Mar 23 17:48:27 gsc systemd[1]: containers-storage-overlay.mount: Deactivated successfully.
```

Cleanup appears to be running successfully, without error. Interesting.

@Luap99 Have you seen anything like this before?
I might have a reason for this - it looks like the containers are running with no-root-pivot which is impacts teardown behaviour.
